message:
refactor(tui): unify ProgramConfig construction across all launch paths (1mfw3.2.1)

All three TUI paths (standard, macro recording, macro playback) now
share a single ProgramConfig + Program::with_config entry point.
Previously playback and standard paths used ftui::App builder which
constructed its own config, creating drift risk. Also fixes opencode
diagnostic path to use actual XDG data dir instead of ~/.opencode.

- 73 lines → 33 lines in run_tui_ftui
- Eliminated dead ProgramConfig for playback/standard paths
- New config fields automatically apply to all paths

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mag","title":"Replace e2e_install_easy fake binaries with real install flow","description":"Eliminate fake rustc/cargo/sha256sum stubs in tests/e2e_install_easy.rs.\n\nDetails:\n- Run install.sh against a real rustup toolchain in a temp HOME (CI + local opt-in).\n- Capture full install logs and verify binary checksums.\n- Keep a fast-path smoke test if needed, but remove fake-bin allowlist entry.","acceptance_criteria":"1) e2e_install_easy runs install.sh with real rustup/cargo in temp HOME.
2) Logs capture stdout/stderr and checksum verification.
3) Fake-bin allowlist entry removed (unless documented exception).
4) Test can be gated by env for local runs; always runs in CI.","notes":"Notes:
- Prefer a dedicated CI job for real install flow.
- Ensure cleanup does not delete user data.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T18:13:23.114573078Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T19:43:06.963139681Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T19:43:06.963000452Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mag","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-ul61","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:00:40Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3","title":"FrankenTUI UX revelation pass for cass TUI","description":"FrankenTUI UX Revelation Program for cass.

Why this exists:
The current cass TUI has strong fundamentals (virtualized rendering, adaptive degradation, robust tests), but still under-leverages FrankenTUI's differentiators: explainable runtime decisions, evidence-driven interaction UX, and regime-aware responsiveness under input/resize stress.

Strategic intent:
1. Make the UI feel materially more responsive under real-world terminal conditions.
2. Make adaptive behavior debuggable and explainable in-product.
3. Raise interaction quality so command, resize, and pointer behavior are stable and predictable.
4. Keep implementation decisions auditable via tests and explicit acceptance criteria.

Program structure:
- .1 Command palette migration to evidence-driven FrankenTUI patterns.
- .2 BOCPD resize coalescer + decision evidence surfaced in cass UI.
- .3 Explainability cockpit overlay/paneling (diff/resize/budget/timeline).
- .4 Structural responsive layouts by breakpoint topology.
- .5 Input smoothness pass (event coalescing + hover stabilization) [already completed].
- .6 Program-level hardening + release gates.

Dependency policy:
- Runtime evidence plumbing (.2) must precede full cockpit timeline capabilities (.3).
- Palette migration (.1) and responsive layouts (.4) can progress in parallel.
- Hardening/release gate (.6) blocks final close of this epic.

Success definition:
- Core workstreams .1 .2 .3 .4 implemented with green quality gates.
- .6 hardening confirms no regressions in stability/perf/usability.
- New behavior is self-explanatory through inspector/cockpit signals.
- Beads are sufficient for future contributors without external planning docs.","design":"Architecture principle: observable adaptivity.

Every adaptive decision (diff/resize/budget/interaction) should be represented as:
1) deterministic runtime behavior,
2) evidence that can be surfaced in UI,
3) tests that lock in expected behavior.

This avoids black-box UX drift and enables root-cause debugging directly from cass.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:10:24.570821358Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-07T23:00:33.418607016Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":201,"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Program note: prioritize functional responsiveness and explainability over visual novelty. If a proposed UI change looks flashy but does not improve operator speed, stability, or debuggability, it is out of scope for this epic. The highest leverage is in making adaptive behavior legible and trustworthy.","created_at":"2026-02-07T23:00:33Z"}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.1","title":"Migrate cass command palette to FrankenTUI evidence-driven palette","description":"Workstream .1: Migrate cass command palette to FrankenTUI evidence-driven palette.

Background:
cass currently uses a local PaletteState + custom overlay renderer. This delivers basic filtering/navigation but lacks explainability, rich match-mode controls, and built-in search quality telemetry. FrankenTUI already ships command-palette primitives with Bayesian evidence and match-type filters.

Goals:
1) Replace custom overlay behavior with ftui command_palette capabilities where practical.
2) Preserve cass action semantics and shortcut discoverability.
3) Expose match evidence and query-latency signals so users can trust ranking output.
4) Maintain deterministic keyboard-first operation and low-latency feel.

In scope:
- Action model mapping from current PaletteAction catalog.
- Rendering + interaction migration in app overlay path.
- Match filter controls and evidence ledger UI.
- Regression tests for palette behavior.

Out of scope:
- Unrelated command renaming churn.
- New non-cass commands not tied to current operator workflows.

Validation expectations:
- Existing palette tests continue to pass or are replaced with stronger equivalents.
- Manual checks confirm no input lag and no focus-trap regressions.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:10:35.263824178Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-07T23:00:34.283183425Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.1","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:10:35.263824178Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":203,"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Implementation guidance: keep the migration adapter-centric. Avoid scattering palette-specific branching throughout unrelated app update/render paths. Preserve all existing action semantics first, then add evidence/filter enhancements in incremental steps to minimize regression surface.","created_at":"2026-02-07T23:00:34Z"}]}
-{"id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.1.1","title":"Palette migration discovery and action mapping contract","description":"Subtask .1.1

Objective:
Create an explicit mapping between current cass palette semantics and FrankenTUI command_palette concepts so migration is deliberate rather than ad hoc.

Deliverables:
- Action inventory with grouping (theme/filter/saved-view/analytics/screenshot/macro/sources).
- Mapping table to target action identifiers and display metadata.
- Interaction contract for keyboard navigation, focus trap behavior, and close/execute semantics.

Rationale:
A clear mapping contract de-risks migration by preventing accidental command loss, hint regressions, or semantic drift.

Done when:
All current palette actions are mapped and no action is left without an explicit target behavior.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:55:43.298717896Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-07T22:55:43.298717896Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.1.1","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:55:43.298717896Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.1.2","title":"Implement palette adapter layer for cass action dispatch","description":"Subtask .1.2

Objective:
Introduce a focused adapter translating FrankenTUI palette selection events into existing cass message/action dispatch, preserving business semantics.

Deliverables:
- Adapter structs/functions that bridge palette result -> CassMsg or equivalent action path.
- Compatibility handling for slot-based actions and existing shortcuts.
- Clear extension points for future palette actions.

Rationale:
A dedicated adapter isolates migration complexity and prevents palette widget internals from leaking into broad app logic.

Done when:
Palette action execution parity is maintained with minimal changes to downstream command handlers.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:55:57.119725562Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-07T22:59:54.413734603Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.1.2","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:55:57.119725562Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.1.2","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:59:54.413671666Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.1.3","title":"Replace custom palette overlay rendering with ftui command_palette widget","description":"Subtask .1.3

Objective:
Swap manual overlay rendering path to use FrankenTUI command_palette rendering primitives while preserving cass visual language.

Deliverables:
- Overlay rendering integration in app view path.
- Selection/highlight/scroll behavior delegated to widget logic.
- Styling alignment with cass theme system.

Rationale:
Using battle-tested widget behavior reduces bespoke rendering bugs and unlocks advanced palette capabilities.

Done when:
The palette opens/renders/navigates through widget-backed code rather than manual row formatting.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:56:06.050805507Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-07T22:59:54.695380531Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.1.3","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:56:06.050805507Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.1.3","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:59:54.695325368Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.1.4","title":"Add match-type filters and explicit filter controls in palette","description":"Subtask .1.4

Objective:
Expose match-mode filtering controls (all/exact/prefix/word-start/substring/fuzzy) in cass palette UX.

Deliverables:
- Keyboard controls for filter mode cycle and direct mode selection.
- Visual indicator of active filter mode.
- Graceful behavior when filters produce zero results.

Rationale:
Filter controls make ranking behavior transparent and increase precision for expert users.

Done when:
Users can intentionally select match modes and observe deterministic result set changes.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:56:22.358901842Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-07T22:59:54.981454650Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.1.4","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:56:22.358901842Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.1.4","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:59:54.981368790Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.1.5","title":"Expose palette ranking evidence ledger in UI","description":"Subtask .1.5

Objective:
Render a compact evidence ledger for selected palette results so users can inspect why a command ranked where it did.

Deliverables:
- Evidence summary panel for selected result.
- Human-readable components (match type, score contributors, confidence hints).
- Empty-state messaging when evidence is unavailable.

Rationale:
Explainability builds operator trust and reduces perceived randomness in fuzzy search.

Done when:
Palette ranking can be inspected in-product without external logs.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:56:22.450954880Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-07T22:59:55.275942695Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.1.5","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:56:22.450954880Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.1.5","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:59:55.275874709Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.1.6","title":"Palette migration regression tests and deterministic behavior lock-in","description":"Objective:
Build comprehensive automated test coverage for palette migration with both unit-level guarantees and end-to-end behavior validation.

Deliverables:
- Extensive unit tests for open/close, navigation, filtering, execution dispatch, and deterministic ordering behavior.
- Dedicated e2e script(s) exercising palette lifecycle flows (open -> type -> filter mode -> navigate -> execute -> close).
- Structured detailed logging from e2e runs (JSONL or equivalent) including query, match_count, selected_action, latency, and assertion outcomes.
- Human-readable test summary artifact linking failed assertions to exact scenario steps.

Rationale:
Palette is a high-frequency operator surface; reliability and debuggability must be proven at both logic and interaction layers.

Done when:
Unit tests and e2e scripts both pass consistently, with rich logs sufficient for fast post-failure diagnosis.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:56:22.959385335Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-07T23:06:51.492830905Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.1.6","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:56:22.959385335Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.1.6","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.1.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:59:57.406086560Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.1.6","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.1.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:59:58.383079912Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.1.6","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.1.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:59:58.693489133Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.1.7","title":"Add palette query latency instrumentation and micro-bench toggle","description":"Objective:
Add low-overhead latency instrumentation for palette query updates and an opt-in micro-bench mode to detect performance regressions.

Deliverables:
- Last-query latency capture.
- Optional benchmark mode with throughput signal (queries/sec).
- Visual budget indicator for query responsiveness.

Rationale:
Making latency visible helps prevent UX regressions as command catalog/features grow.

Done when:
Operators can quickly verify palette responsiveness from within the TUI.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:56:23.007851279Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-07T22:59:55.597488555Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.1.7","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:56:23.007851279Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.1.7","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:59:55.597408966Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.2","title":"Enable BOCPD resize coalescer and surface resize decisions in UI","description":"Workstream .2: Enable BOCPD resize coalescer and surface resize decisions in UI.

Background:
cass currently runs on ftui runtime with budget + mouse enabled, but does not fully wire advanced ProgramConfig knobs for resize evidence and regime-aware coalescing. FrankenTUI includes a resize coalescer with BOCPD support and decision logs.

Goals:
1) Wire ProgramConfig with explicit resize coalescer settings (logging + BOCPD).
2) Capture/summarize resize decisions in cass-facing model state.
3) Surface resize behavior in inspector so users can diagnose resize storms.
4) Keep behavior deterministic and robust across fullscreen/inline/macro paths.

In scope:
- run_tui_ftui config unification and upgrade.
- Evidence plumbing and summary model.
- Inspector presentation of resize regime/action/evidence.
- Test coverage for resize storms and fallback behavior.

Out of scope:
- Full explainability cockpit UX (tracked in .3).

Validation expectations:
- No regressions in macro record/playback paths.
- Resize behavior remains stable under rapid terminal changes.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:10:35.384452802Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-07T23:00:34.870097395Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.2","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:10:35.384452802Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":204,"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Implementation guidance: treat runtime config parity as non-negotiable. Any knob added for standard mode must be reviewed for record/playback + inline/fullscreen paths. Evidence wiring should fail soft (graceful empty state) rather than fail closed (broken UI path).","created_at":"2026-02-07T23:00:34Z"}]}
-{"id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.2.1","title":"Unify ProgramConfig construction across standard, record, and playback paths","description":"Objective:
Refactor run_tui_ftui so all execution modes share one explicit ProgramConfig construction path.

Deliverables:
- Common config builder with mode-specific deltas only where required.
- Consistent runtime knobs across standard, macro recording, and playback paths.
- Reduced configuration drift risk.

Why this matters:
Without a unified builder, advanced runtime options are easy to wire in one path and forget in others, creating invisible behavioral inconsistency.

Done when:
All launch paths consume the same config baseline and differences are intentional and documented.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:56:34.183111790Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-07T22:56:34.183111790Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.2.1","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:56:34.183111790Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.2.2","title":"Enable BOCPD + logging CoalescerConfig profile for cass runtime","description":"Objective:
Select and wire an explicit resize coalescer profile with BOCPD enabled and decision logging turned on.

Deliverables:
- CoalescerConfig settings for steady/burst/deadline behavior.
- BOCPD enabled with sane defaults for cass interaction profile.
- Logging configuration suitable for UI-facing evidence summaries.

Why this matters:
Regime-aware resize behavior is core to responsiveness under resize storms; logging is required for explainability and debugging.

Done when:
Runtime is using explicit BOCPD/logging settings in all relevant launch modes.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:56:47.528311592Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-07T22:59:59.009769383Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.2.2","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:56:47.528311592Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.2.2","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:59:59.009687150Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.2.3","title":"Wire resize decision evidence capture path into cass model layer","description":"Objective:
Capture resize decision/evidence signals emitted by runtime and make them available to cass model state.

Deliverables:
- Evidence ingestion hook(s) from runtime output.
- Parsing/normalization into typed cass-side structures.
- Error-tolerant handling of missing/partial evidence events.

Why this matters:
Evidence must exist in model memory before any inspector/cockpit panel can explain adaptive behavior.

Done when:
cass model can access latest resize decision and associated evidence summary during rendering.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:56:55.716818104Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-07T22:59:59.331809786Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.2.3","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:56:55.716818104Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.2.3","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:59:59.331718115Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.2.4","title":"Define cass resize evidence summary state and rendering-ready schema","description":"Objective:
Design minimal but sufficient cass-side state for resize evidence presentation.

Deliverables:
- Structs for latest action/regime/timing/evidence contributions.
- Ring buffer or snapshot policy for recent resize decisions.
- Backfill/default semantics when evidence is absent.

Why this matters:
A stable local schema decouples UI rendering from raw runtime event shape and supports safe evolution.

Done when:

-{"id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.4","title":"Implement structural responsive layouts for cass search/detail surfaces","description":"Workstream .4: Implement structural responsive layouts for cass search/detail surfaces.

Background:
cass already switches among narrow/medium/wide categories, but still relies on mostly handcrafted split logic. FrankenTUI responsive patterns provide cleaner breakpoint-driven layout topology and visibility rules.

Goals:
1) Move from ad-hoc layout branching toward structured breakpoint topology.
2) Improve UX continuity across terminal widths (especially medium + narrow).
3) Preserve reliable hit regions/focus behavior while changing layout structure.

In scope:
- Breakpoint contract and topology definitions.
- Responsive layout updates for search/detail and analytics sections.
- Visibility rules for pane-specific affordances.
- Regression tests over size matrix and hit-region invariants.

Out of scope:
- Cosmetic-only changes without structural UX gains.

Validation expectations:
- Narrow/medium/wide behavior is explicit and test-verified.
- Mouse/focus routing remains correct in all breakpoint topologies.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:10:35.885723324Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-07T23:03:18.910716275Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.4","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:10:35.885723324Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":202,"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Design note: responsive behavior must be structural, not cosmetic. Breakpoint logic should define topology and interaction affordance availability, not only label truncation or spacing tweaks.  {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.4.1","title":"Define breakpoint topology matrix for search/detail/analytics surfaces","description":"Objective:
Codify breakpoint-specific topology (pane arrangement, visibility, navigation assumptions) as explicit contract before implementation changes.

- Narrow/medium/wide topology matrix.
- Per-surface layout intent notes.
- Interaction expectations (focus/mouse/hit region implications).

Why this matters:
A shared topology contract prevents fragmented behavior and hidden edge-case drift.

Done when:
All responsive work can reference one explicit breakpoint blueprint.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:57:55.554498762Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-07T23:24:13.758100426Z","closed_at":"2026-02-07T23:24:13.758075991Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.4.1","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:57:55.554498762Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.4.2","title":"Refactor search surface to structural responsive layout primitives","description":"Objective:
Refactor search/results/detail surface to use explicit structural responsive primitives instead of ad-hoc breakpoint branching.

Deliverables:
- Structured layout split definitions by breakpoint.

Structural responsiveness improves maintainability and reduces logic bugs in medium/narrow transitions.

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.4.3","title":"Implement responsive visibility policy for controls, hints, and split affordances","description":"Objective:
Define and enforce visibility rules for optional controls/hints/handles per breakpoint and degradation context.

Deliverables:
- Visibility matrix for non-essential UI elements.
- Consistent hide/show logic with clear rationale.
- Safeguards against inaccessible actions when elements are hidden.

Why this matters:
Responsive UX quality depends on removing clutter without removing capability.

Done when:
Visibility behavior is intentional, testable, and documented in-code.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:58:14.038448628Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T00:03:13.582105361Z","closed_at":"2026-02-08T00:03:13.582079223Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.4.3","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:58:14.038448628Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.4.3","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.4.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-07T23:00:05.984152806Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.4.3","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.4.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-07T23:00:06.216534068Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.4.4","title":"Add ultra-narrow terminal fallback UX policy","description":"Objective:
Define safe behavior for very constrained terminals (e.g., <= 60 cols or low height) to prevent unusable layouts.

Deliverables:
- Minimal viable layout mode and truncation rules.
- Priority ordering for content and controls in constrained space.
- No-panic behavior for extreme dimensions.

Why this matters:
Robust terminal apps degrade gracefully even under unusually small dimensions.

Done when:
Ultra-narrow scenarios remain functional and stable.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:58:14.486515561Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-07T23:47:33.751226626Z","closed_at":"2026-02-07T23:47:33.751203603Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.4.4","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:58:14.486515561Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.4.4","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.4.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-07T23:00:06.938671845Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.4.4","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.4.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-07T23:00:07.174002333Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

-{"id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.4.7","title":"Responsive layout regression suite across size matrix and interaction invariants","description":"Objective:
Create comprehensive tests that validate responsive behavior across representative terminal sizes and interaction pathways.

Deliverables:
- Unit tests for topology selection, pane-visibility policy, and hit-region/focus invariants.
- E2e size-sweep scripts (small/medium/wide/very large, including ultra-narrow fallback) with keyboard and mouse interaction flows.
- Structured logging artifacts for each scenario (resolved topology, visible panes, focus transitions, hit-target map, assertion outcomes, timings).
- Diffable regression summaries highlighting behavior changes by breakpoint.

Why this matters:
Responsive logic is high-risk for subtle regressions; broad, deterministic unit+e2e coverage with logs is essential for trust.

Done when:
Responsive behavior is locked in by comprehensive automated coverage and actionable diagnostics.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:58:15.068136009Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T00:02:22.792533235Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.4.7","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:58:15.068136009Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.4.7","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.4.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-07T23:00:07.414365619Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.4.7","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.4.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-07T23:00:07.664953474Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.4.7","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.4.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-07T23:00:07.915432798Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.4.7","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.4.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-07T23:00:08.248432103Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.4.7","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.4.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-07T23:00:08.482471786Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.5","title":"Add input smoothness pass (event coalescing + hover stabilization UX)","description":"Workstream .5: Input smoothness pass (event coalescing + hover stabilization UX).

Status note:

Completed intent:
- Reduce pointer jitter and drag noise.
- Improve predictability of hover/selection interactions.
- Add focused tests to lock in behavior.

Program dependency context:
Downstream work should assume this baseline exists and build on it rather than re-solving the same interaction stability layer.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:10:35.952817550Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-07T22:54:58.624889833Z","closed_at":"2026-02-07T22:49:00.071932518Z","close_reason":"Completed: drag jitter filter + hover stabilization, 6 tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.5","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:10:35.952817550Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.6","title":"Program hardening and release gates for FrankenTUI UX overhaul","description":"Workstream .6: program-level hardening, integration validation, and release closure for the FrankenTUI UX overhaul.

Purpose:
Even strong feature work can regress UX quality if cross-workstream interactions are not validated holistically. This workstream ensures the combined system is stable, fast, and operationally understandable before epic closure.

In scope:
- Cross-workstream integration checks.
- Quality gate execution and bug-fix loop.
- Performance and reliability regression detection.
- Comprehensive unit-test and e2e script execution with structured logging artifacts for auditability.
- Final operator-facing documentation and closeout criteria.

Out of scope:
- Net-new feature expansion unrelated to closure quality.

Success signal:
All prerequisite workstreams are integrated, quality gates are green, test/e2e logs are complete, and closure notes provide clear handoff context for future contributors.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:55:30.455380974Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T00:05:32.778845681Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.6","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:55:30.455380974Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":206,"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.6","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Closeout guidance: do not close this workstream based solely on passing unit tests. Require integrated manual scenarios and performance sanity checks. Any unresolved UX ambiguity should become explicit follow-up beads before epic closure.","created_at":"2026-02-07T23:00:36Z"}]}
-{"id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.6.1","title":"Assemble cross-workstream integration checklist and execution plan","description":"Objective:
Create one integration checklist early that validates interactions among palette, resize evidence, cockpit, and responsive layout changes, and translates them into executable test runs.

Deliverables:
- Cross-workstream scenario matrix (normal, stress, edge) with explicit expected outcomes.
- Mapping from scenarios to required unit suites and e2e scripts across .1/.2/.3/.4 workstreams.
- Canonical structured-log schema for integration/e2e runs (scenario_id, mode, event stream, timing, assertion outcomes).
- Triage playbook for first-failure diagnosis and ownership of each unresolved gap.

Why this matters:
Cross-feature interactions are where subtle regressions hide; defining execution and logging contracts up front prevents late-stage ambiguity.

Done when:
The checklist is executable, repeatable, and directly consumed by .6.2 and .6.5 validation runs.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:58:27.182138860Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T00:02:52.508594925Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.6.1","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:58:27.182138860Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.6.2","title":"Run quality-gate sweep and resolve all blockers for UX overhaul branch state","description":"Objective:
Run mandatory quality gates and drive all discovered issues to resolution for the integrated overhaul state.

Deliverables:
- cargo check --all-targets clean.
- cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings clean.
- cargo fmt --check clean.
- Comprehensive unit-test sweep for palette, resize evidence, cockpit, and responsive layout workstreams.
- Deterministic e2e script execution per integration checklist with structured run logs captured for each scenario.
- Failure triage notes linking each broken assertion to root-cause bead/fix.

Why this matters:
Foundational quality gates plus explicit unit/e2e verification prevent integration debt and silent behavior drift.

Done when:
All quality gates and required unit/e2e suites are green, with detailed logs available for audit and regression comparison.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:58:43.265644634Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T00:02:59.362691287Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.6.2","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.1.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T00:02:54.562763016Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.6.2","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.2.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T00:02:56.468767067Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.6.2","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.2.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T00:02:57.920779470Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.6.2","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.3.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T00:02:58.924458745Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.6.2","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.4.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T00:02:59.362639200Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.6.2","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:58:43.265644634Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.6.2","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.6.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-07T23:00:10.644843285Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.6.3","title":"Update operator-facing docs and in-repo rationale for new UX behavior","description":"Objective:
Document the new runtime/inspector/palette/responsive behavior so future contributors understand intent without external planning artifacts.

Deliverables:
- Updated internal documentation snippets relevant to changed behavior.
- Notes on tradeoffs and intentional design decisions.
- Pointers to key test coverage and diagnostics.

Why this matters:
Self-documenting systems reduce future rework and repeated design debates.

Done when:
Future contributors can reason about the overhaul from repository artifacts alone.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:58:43.364062474Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T00:03:02.610536013Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.6.3","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:58:43.364062474Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.6.3","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.6.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-07T23:00:11.377007918Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.6.3","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.6.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T00:03:02.610415739Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.6.3","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.6.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-07T23:00:11.613739719Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.6.4","title":"Run UBS scan and resolve safety/reliability findings","description":"Objective:
Run UBS on changed files and close out any substantial safety, reliability, or bug-risk findings.

Deliverables:
- UBS scan output review.
- Fixes for high-impact findings.
- Follow-up beads for deferred lower-priority findings (if any).

Why this matters:
Independent static review catches classes of issues that targeted tests may miss.

Done when:
UBS findings are either resolved or explicitly tracked with rationale.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:58:43.678488755Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T00:03:02.114614718Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.6.4","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:58:43.678488755Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.6.4","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.6.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-07T23:00:11.136230480Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.6.4","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.6.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T00:03:02.114501507Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
